title: Colors, Fonts & Visual Identity
objectives: Apply colors using named colors, hex codes, and RGB values
Set font families, sizes, and weights to establish visual hierarchy
Understand the cascade — how CSS rules are applied and overridden
hints: Colors can be named ('crimson'), hex (#DC143C), or rgb(220, 20, 60) — they all work
Use font-family with fallbacks: font-family: 'Georgia', 'Times New Roman', serif
More specific selectors override less specific ones — an ID beats a class beats an element
assessmentCriteria: Stylesheet defines a body background color and text color
At least two different font families are used
Colors are applied using at least two different formats (hex, rgb, named)
Headings have distinct styling from body text
